ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF. Certificate is a 4-page file: pages 1-2 are ORIGINAL (1/2 and 2/2), pages 3-4 are COPY (1/2 and 2/2). All pages contain identical information. Verification based on ORIGINAL (pages 1-2).

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: High quality text-based PDF, 3 pages. All text clearly legible. Invoice issued by Volvo Bus Corporation.

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: The certificate contains 'LC # 307-01-0284989' in box 5 (Remarks). 'LC #' is a separate label followed by a number, constituting a clear Letter of Credit reference with bank-term context. This is NOT a substring within a reference number — it is a standalone field label 'LC #' followed by the LC number '307-01-0284989'. The invoice confirms this LC reference in the 'Customer Reference' field ('L/C No.: 307-01-0284') and 'Seller reference' field ('L/C No.: 307-01-0284989 PO no : 250012605'). LC exception is activated per section 2.2.

criteria_met: Consignor 'Volvo Bus Corporation' identified as invoice issuer 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION'
Consignee 'PT. INDOTRUCK UTAMA' identified as Buyer and Ship-to in invoice
LC reference 307-01-0284989 found in invoice seller reference
pairing_validation.reasoning: All three pairing criteria are met: consignor matches issuer, consignee matches buyer/ship-to, and LC reference is confirmed. Document pair is valid.

checkpoint_verifications.certificate_value: Volvo Bus Corporation, Bussgatan 1, 50464 Borås, Sweden

rules_applied.rule_description: LC exception active – consignor verification not required

checkpoint_verifications.motivation: LC exception (section 2.2) is active. Consignor verification is not required under LC rules. Checkpoint set to NOT_APPLICABLE.
checkpoint_verifications.debug_explanation: Certificate contains 'LC # 307-01-0284989' in box 5, which is a valid LC reference with a separate field label. Per section 2.2, when LC exception is active, only country of origin shall be verified. All other checkpoints are NOT_APPLICABLE.

checkpoint_verifications.invoice_value: Country of origin: SE (page 1 header); COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN (page 3 additional information)

rules_applied.rule_description: LC exception – only country of origin is verified
Normalization of country names – SE = Sweden
EU normalization – European Community treated as EU
EU member state normalization – Sweden is EU member

comparison_steps.result: Certificate box 3 states: 'The european community Sweden'. This indicates two origins: (1) European Community (EU), and (2) Sweden specifically.

comparison_steps.description: Identify explicit origin fields in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice page 1 header contains 'Country of origin: SE'. Invoice page 3 additional information contains 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN'. Both are explicit, dedicated origin fields.
Certificate states 'Sweden'. Invoice states 'SE' (page 1) and 'SWEDEN' (page 3). 'SE' is the ISO alpha-2 code for Sweden. After normalization per 4.5.4, SE = Sweden. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Verify 'European Community' from certificate against invoice
comparison_steps.result: Certificate states 'The european community'. Per 4.5.4.1, European Community = EU. Per 4.5.4.2, Sweden (SE) is an EU member state. Since the invoice confirms origin as Sweden/SE, and Sweden is an EU member state, the EU component is verified. No non-EU origins are present in the invoice. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Check for contradictory origins in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice contains only 'SE' / 'SWEDEN' as origin. No non-EU origins present. No contradiction.

checkpoint_verifications.motivation: Certificate states origin as 'The european community Sweden'. Invoice explicitly states 'Country of origin: SE' (page 1 header) and 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN' (page 3 additional information). Both are dedicated origin fields. Sweden = SE after ISO code normalization (4.5.4). European Community is verified as Sweden is an EU member state (4.5.4.1, 4.5.4.2). No non-EU origins in invoice. Both origin components verified. MATCH.
checkpoint_verifications.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'The european community Sweden'. Invoice origin fields: 'Country of origin: SE' (header, page 1) and 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN' (page 3 additional info). Step 1: 'Sweden' in certificate → 'SE' and 'SWEDEN' in invoice → SE is ISO-2 for Sweden → MATCH via 4.5.4. Step 2: 'European Community' → EU per 4.5.4.1 → Sweden is EU-27 member → invoice confirms Sweden origin → EU component verified via 4.5.4.2. No non-EU origins detected in invoice. Result: MATCH with high confidence.

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: Certificate box 5 contains 'LC # 307-01-0284989'. 'LC #' is a separate field label (not a substring of a reference number). It is followed by a concrete number. The invoice confirms this LC in 'Seller reference: L/C No.: 307-01-0284989'. LC exception is activated. Only country of origin will be verified; all other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE.

File 1 (Certificate of Origin) contains 4 pages: pages 1-2 ORIGINAL, pages 3-4 COPY. File 2 (Invoice) contains 3 pages of invoice SEA9000027. Certificate file is identified as certificate, invoice file as invoice. Using ORIGINAL certificate (pages 1-2) and invoice from file 2.

Criterion 1: LC reference 307-01-0284989 in certificate matches seller reference in invoice. Criterion 2: Consignor 'Volvo Bus Corporation' matches invoice issuer 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION'. Criterion 3: Consignee 'PT. INDOTRUCK UTAMA' matches invoice buyer. All three criteria met.
SE (ISO alpha-2) normalized to Sweden. Certificate 'Sweden' matches invoice 'SE'/'SWEDEN'.
'The european community' in certificate = EU. Sweden is EU member state (EU-27). Invoice confirms Sweden as origin. EU component verified.
Certificate states 'European Community Sweden'. Invoice states 'SE'/'SWEDEN'. Sweden is EU member. Per 4.5.4.2, EU ↔ member state equivalence applies. Both components verified.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: LC reference might not be a genuine Letter of Credit
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate uses 'LC #' as a separate label in box 5 (Remarks), followed by number 307-01-0284989. The invoice confirms this as 'L/C No.: 307-01-0284989' in the seller reference field, using explicit bank-term 'L/C No.'. This is unambiguously a Letter of Credit reference, not a coincidental substring.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Origin might not match because certificate says 'European Community' while invoice says 'SE'
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per 4.5.4.1, 'European Community' = EU. Per 4.5.4.2, SE (Sweden) is an EU member state. The invoice also explicitly states 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN' on page 3. Both EU and Sweden components are verified.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet innehåller en hänvisning till Letter of Credit (LC), vilket innebär att verifieringen begränsas till ursprungsland. Certifikatet anger ursprungsland 'The european community Sweden' och fakturan bekräftar ursprungsland som 'SE' och 'SWEDEN' i dedikerade ursprungsfält. Alla verifierade uppgifter överensstämmer.
overall_assessment.reasoning_summary: LC exception activated based on 'LC # 307-01-0284989' in certificate box 5. Per section 2.2, only country of origin is verified. Certificate states origin as 'The european community Sweden'. Invoice explicitly confirms 'Country of origin: SE' (page 1) and 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN' (page 3). Sweden = SE via ISO normalization. European Community verified via EU member state equivalence (Sweden is EU-27 member). All applicable checkpoints: MATCH. Result: IDENTICAL.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura
human_readable_report.document_pair_summary: Certifikat SEG-25D-380119 (Original, 2 sidor) utfärdat 2025-10-22 av West Sweden Chamber of Commerce, för Volvo Bus Corporation (Sverige) till PT. Indotruck Utama (Indonesien). Faktura SEA9000027, daterad 2025-10-17, utställd av Volvo Bus Corporation. Avser 25 enheter Volvo B11R 6X2.
human_readable_report.lc_status: LC-undantag AKTIVERAT. Certifikatet innehåller LC-referens '307-01-0284989' i ruta 5. Fakturan bekräftar detta i 'Seller reference: L/C No.: 307-01-0284989'. Enbart ursprungsland verifieras.

sections.invoice_value: Country of origin: SE (sida 1); COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN (sida 3)
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'The european community Sweden'. Fakturan anger 'Country of origin: SE' (sida 1) och 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN : SWEDEN' (sida 3), båda i dedikerade ursprungsfält. SE = Sweden (ISO alfa-2). European Community = EU; Sverige är EU-medlemsstat. Båda ursprungskomponenterna verifierade. MATCH.

human_readable_report.conclusion: Samtliga tillämpliga kontrollpunkter verifierade som MATCH. LC-undantag aktivt – enbart ursprungsland verifierat. Ursprungsland 'The european community Sweden' bekräftat via 'SE' och 'SWEDEN' i fakturan. Rekommendation: Automatiskt godkännande.

output_quality_checks.notes: All applicable checkpoints verified. Only 4.5 (Country of Origin) is applicable due to LC exception. Status MATCH with confidence 0.97. comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent with all applicable checkpoints being MATCH.
